domain: Madagascar ravinala traveler's palm (Ravenala madagascariensis): leaves in single planar east-west fan; each leaf base petiole cups up to 1 quart water as drought reserve; orthogonal axes (fan + cup) don't interfere; passive accumulation no active pumping.
specific_mechanism: Single-axis planar fan for primary function + orthogonal off-axis reservoir for stress reserve; two functional axes orthogonal; off-band capacity for memory hoard.
llm_application: RAVINALA orthogonal basin-stable KV reserve: augment standard KV cache with SECONDARY ORTHOGONAL RESERVE-BASIN. For each KV vector kv_t, compute reserve_KV_t = (I - Proj_U) kv_t where U = top-k principal direction of cache. When memory budget pressure forces token eviction, EVICTED tokens preserve their reserve_KV in low-rank orthogonal storage. On retrieval requiring evicted token, REHYDRATE primary_KV ≈ Proj_U(prior_value) + reserve_KV. Reserve persists at basin-stable low-rank size. Differs from (a) standard eviction (drops entirely), (b) KV quantization (lossy uniform compression), (c) MTDS hierarchical (multi-tier copy not decomposition), (d) PagedAttention (paging), (e) Streaming-LLM attention sink (keep positions). Recombines (i) ORTHOGONAL DECOMPOSITION + (ii) RESERVE BASIN for evicted tokens + (iii) REHYDRATION via projection sum.
novelty_claim: Standard KV eviction drops tokens. KV quantization lossy uniform. MTDS hierarchical. PagedAttention paging. RAVINALA recombines (a) ORTHOGONAL decomposition + (b) RESERVE basin for evicted + (c) REHYDRATION sum. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
